top_signals.title: Treasuries Gain Ahead of Week’s First Batch of Employment Data
top_signals.summary: Treasuries hovered near a three-week high ahead of this week’s first batch of jobs numbers that could cement the case for the Federal Reserve to deliver an interest-rate hike under new Chair Kevin Warsh.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: China’s hunt for US tungsten escalates global critical minerals race
top_signals.summary: Effort has driven up prices and prompted calls to restrict sales of a vital national security resource to overseas buyers
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Statistical Notice 2026/04 - BEEDS User Acceptance Testing (UAT) Environment – Statistical Taxonomy v1.3.1 FINAL
top_signals.summary: Statistical Notices update the definitions and guidance contained in the Banking Statistics Yellow Folder
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Modernising money and markets − speech by Sarah Breeden
US rice farmers spy a lifeline in crisis-hit Cuba
top_signals.summary: Opening up of the communist island could result in a ‘big shot in the arm’ for planters in the southern US

top_signals.title: US may consider placing nukes in Poland, Baltic States, report says
top_signals.summary: While the discussions were ongoing, an agreement to expand U.S. nuclear hosting was not imminent, an unnamed source told the FT.
